_doc: Multi-query gate facets (template). Copy to profile/facets.json and fill from YOUR profile, or generate with `node --env-file=.env scoring/gen-facets.mjs`. The gate score (jobs.semantic) = mean of the top-2 POSITIVE facet similarities. The 'negative' and 'skills' facets are stored for transparency/future weighting but do NOT feed the gate score. facets.json itself is gitignored (it contains your personal background).

facets.name: role-dialect-a
facets.text: ~35-word prose describing one role dialect you target, written like a job-description excerpt (responsibilities + stack + domain). Ground it in roles you actually want.

facets.name: role-dialect-b
facets.text: A distinct second dialect of the same target — distinct enough that a posting written in one would score low against the other (the gate takes the top-2, so near-duplicates waste a slot).

facets.name: role-dialect-c
facets.text: A third distinct dialect (e.g. an infrastructure/platform framing of the same field vs an application/product framing).

facets.name: role-dialect-d
facets.text: A fourth distinct dialect (e.g. a customer-facing / forward-deployed framing).

facets.name: role-dialect-e
facets.text: A fifth distinct dialect (only if genuinely distinct from the others; otherwise use four).

facets.name: avoid-pattern
facets.text: The kind of role you want to AVOID, in its own words (e.g. work that merely USES a tool rather than BUILDING the thing you want to build).

facets.name: skills-background
facets.text: Your real background and seniority/timeline — languages, cloud, data, years — stated honestly. Used for transparency/future weighting; does not feed the gate score.
